shadows.name: glow-focus
shadows.purpose: Focus states on primary actions only.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary actions — submit, confirm, main CTA. Deep navy background, white text.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1e3a5f; color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 20px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0f2744; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgb(30 58 95 / 0.25); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1e3a5f; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary actions — cancel, skip, less prominent choices.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #e5e7eb; color: #111827; padding: 12px 20px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #d1d5db; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1e3a5f; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Tertiary actions — text links, navigation items, low-emphasis choices.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #111827; padding: 12px 20px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #e5e7eb; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1e3a5f; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Content container — papers, gaps, data surfaces. Flat at rest, shadow on hover for interactive cards.
components.html:
Research Gap

Limited evaluation benchmarks for cross-lingual transfer in low-resource settings.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); transition: box-shadow 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1), 0 4px 6px -4px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 600; color: #111827; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 1rem; color: #6b7280; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text input — search, forms, data entry. Subtle border, clean focus state.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 12px 16px; font-size: 0.875rem; background: #f8f9fb; border: 1.5px solid #d1d5db; border-radius: 12px; color: #111827; transition: border-color 0.15s ease, box-shadow 0.15s ease; outline: none; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: #9ca3af; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #1e3a5f; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgb(30 58 95 / 0.15); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9ca3af; }

components.name: Badge
components.description: Status indicator — category tags, status labels, metadata pills.
components.css: .ds-badge-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 4px 12px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; background: #1e3a5f; color: #ffffff; border-radius: 9999px; line-height: 1; }

components.name: Navigation Sidebar
components.description: Primary navigation — deep navy background, white text, active state highlight.
components.html: DashboardPapersResearch Gaps
components.css: .ds-nav { background: #1e3a5f; padding: 16px 0; border-radius: 12px; } .ds-nav-item { display: block; padding: 10px 20px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7); text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; border-radius: 8px; margin: 2px 8px; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); color: #ffffff; } .ds-nav-item.active { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.15); color: #ffffff; font-weight: 600; }

narrative.overview:
GapMiner's design strips away noise to reveal what matters in the research landscape. Like a well-calibrated optical instrument, the interface exists to bring hidden patterns into focus — not to call attention to itself. Every surface, every color, every typographic choice serves comprehension and navigation. The system rejects decoration that doesn't serve understanding.

The aesthetic is scholarly restraint: deep navy carries authority without coldness, warm amber accents signal discovery and warmth like aged paper bindings, and neutral surfaces recede so data stays front-and-center. No glassmorphism, no gradient text, no animated backgrounds. The best design pass removes something, not adds something.
keyCharacteristics: Content-first: every pixel serves comprehension or navigation
Restrained surfaces: flat or subtly elevated, never decorative
Deep navy authority: scholarly without being institutional
Warm accents for discovery: amber signals insight, not decoration
Precision over polish: every component feels deliberate

rules.name: The Lens Rule
rules.body: Colors exist to organize information, not to decorate. If a color doesn't help the user distinguish, prioritize, or act, remove it. The primary navy appears on ≤15% of any given screen; its restraint is its authority.

rules.name: The Warmth Rule
rules.body: Warm amber is reserved for moments of discovery and confirmation — a successful analysis, a highlighted gap, a completed action. It never appears as a background or decorative element. Its scarcity makes it meaningful.

rules.name: The Disappearing Type Rule
rules.body: The reader should notice the content, not the font. If someone comments on the typography, something is wrong. Inter's neutrality is the point — it carries information without calling attention to itself.

rules.name: The Line Length Rule
rules.body: Body text never exceeds 75ch. Research content is dense; giving it room to breathe is not optional. Short line lengths reduce cognitive load and improve comprehension.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as response to state (hover, elevation, focus) or structural need (modals, dropdowns). A card sitting idle should feel grounded, not floating.

dos: Do use deep navy for structural elements (nav, primary buttons, headings) — it carries scholarly authority
Do use warm amber sparingly for moments of discovery
Do keep body text at 4.5:1 contrast minimum against its background
Do use flat surfaces as the default. Shadows are feedback, not decoration
Do cap body line length at 65–75ch for readability
Do use text-wrap: balance on headings and text-wrap: pretty on long prose
Do include @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) alternatives for all animations
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). It's decorative, never meaningful.
Don't use glassmorphism (backdrop-filter blur on cards). It's decorative, not functional.
Don't use glowing borders or animated gradients. These are AI slop tells.
Don't use cream, sand, or warm-tinted body backgrounds.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't pair similar fonts. Inter is the only family; hierarchy comes from weight and size.
Don't animate CSS layout properties unless truly needed.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing. Use ease-out with exponential curves.
Don't put border: 1px solid X + box-shadow with blur ≥ 16px on the same element.
Don't use border-radius: 24px+ on cards. Cards top out at 16px radius.
